text.title: Funnel Tactics Advanced Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Funnel Tactics Advanced` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each personalization segment cell MUST have ≥ 500 weekly users before personalization is applied. Below the floor, personalization overfits and underperforms generic.
rule.rationale: Below 500 users / cell the variance dominates; personalization causes worse results than a single generic variant.

rule.statement: Retargeting sequences MUST cap at 14 days OR 5 impressions per user, whichever first. Open-ended retargeting is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Beyond 14 days the click-through collapses + brand fatigue accumulates; the cap protects the channel.

rule.statement: Exit-intent popups MUST have a 30-day cooldown per visitor. Forcing the same popup on repeat visitors is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Repeat popups cause hard bounces; the cooldown preserves the offer's signalled scarcity.

rule.statement: ICE prioritisation MUST have explicit thresholds: ICE ≥ 24 = test immediately, ICE 18-23 = next-sprint, ICE < 18 = backlog. Discretionary ICE scoring is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without thresholds, ICE scoring degenerates into vibes; thresholds enforce the prioritisation queue.

rule.statement: Event taxonomy (event name + property schema) MUST be pinned in a versioned doc + reviewed quarterly. Ad-hoc events are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc events cause downstream analysis to break; pinned schema + quarterly review keep the data warehouse usable.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
